ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT DEFENDANT WISMER & BRECKER CONTRACTING ENGINEERS'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. WORKERS' COMPENSATION IS NOT PLAINTIFF'S EXCLUSIVE REMEDY BECAUSE PLAINTIFF ALLEGES ASBESTOS EXPOSURE BY THE ACTIVITIES OF DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEES WHILE PLAINTIFF WAS EMPLOYED BY A COMPANY OTHER THAN DEFENDANT. THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. DEFENDANT RELIES SOLELY ON PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND FAILS TO CITE TO ANY "ALL FACTS" INTERROGATORY RESPONSES REGARDING ASBESTOS EXPOSURE. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO CONTINUE HEARING PER CCP 437C(H) WAS DENIED. THE COURT DECLINED TO RULE ON DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIAL OBJECTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. RULING:
Defendant DEFENDANT WISMER & BRECKER CONTRACTING ENGINEERS's motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Motion is denied. Workers' compensation is not plaintiff's exclusive remedy because plaintiff alleges asbestos exposure by the activities of defendant's employees while plaintiff was employed by a company other than defendant. The moving party failed to sustain its burden of production. Defendant relies solely on plaintiff's deposition testimony and fails to cite to any "all facts" interrogatory responses regarding asbestos exposure. Defendant's request to continue hearing per CCP 437c(h) was denied. The Court declined to rule on defendant's evidential objections. Order signed in open court.
